rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type annexes when they describe interface behavior.
Use visible identifiers from the current component scope to avoid unresolved reference errors.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be scoped to their owning component declaration.
Component type contracts should only reference ports and features visible in the component type scope.
Assignments and implementation-level bindings belong in component implementation annexes.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use named values or visible relations from the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions use `assume "label": expression;` and guarantees use `guarantee "label": expression;`.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference subcomponent paths from incompatible component type scopes.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
